title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
prompt: Given a sorted integer array `nums`, remove duplicates in-place so each unique element appears only once, preserving relative order. Return k = the count of unique elements; the first k positions of nums must contain the unique values.
constraints: nums is sorted in non-decreasing order.
hints: Sortedness lets you compare consecutive elements only.
Keep a write pointer for the next unique slot.

optimal.approach: Two pointers: write index w starts at 1; copy when nums[i] !== nums[w-1].

pitfalls: Off-by-one when w starts at 0 vs 1.
Failing to update the value at index w.
